title: Prosopis elephantina
intro: Prosopis elephantina, commonly known as the elephant tree or marula, is a tree species native to southern Africa, characterized by its small, deciduous leaves and white or yellow flowers. The health benefits of Prosopis elephantina include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Prosopis elephantina is often used to make various herbal preparations, such as infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Prosopis elephantina is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the mimosa or acacia family. It belongs to the genus Prosopis, which is a group of thorny trees native to Africa and Asia. The species is native to tropical Africa and is widely distributed across the continent. Prosopis elephantina is a deciduous tree that can grow up to 15 meters tall and has a wide range of uses, including as a source of food, fuel, and medicine. Its classification also includes the subfamily Mimosoideae, which is characterized by its bipinnate leaves and showy flowers.

The plant's leaves and pods are rich in flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
